Effect on funding_hedging is tested in the NEXT stage after manual cp.
two_repo: Developed in template repo ai_project_harness; harness_owned (validate-stage.py, schemas) cp'd DOWN to funding_hedging after acceptance (manual, per two-repo rule). Do not mix into a funding_hedging stage branch.

design_decisions_resolution.D1: whitelist in validator source (assertion_id enum); v1 = only class-1 review_fingerprint_trails_status; class-2 (exempt verdict==ACCEPT) NOT in v1; negative-list never-exemptable (fingerprint-recompute, clean worktree, reviewer identity :717-718, exception record's own evidence+integrity).
design_decisions_resolution.D2: authorized_exceptions:[{assertion_id, scope, applies_to_fingerprint (harden 1: pinned to exact fingerprint, auto-expires on next fix), reason, authorizer=='user' literal (harden 2: evidence_file must contain verbatim user authorization), evidence_file (must exist, :735-737 pattern), at}]; PASS-with-exception is explicitly printed, never silent; no expiry field (pinning is inherently one-shot).
design_decisions_resolution.D3: chain+prefix coverage: tasks[] head-to-tail linked (task0.base==status.base, task[i+1].base==task[i].head, last.head==status.head); review_k.diff_fingerprint == recomputed status.base..task[j].head prefix (j=covers_through_task); tasks after j need own review OR class-1 exception; degenerate single-task == current behavior; tasks[] absent == current single-fingerprint assertion.
